question: Is the distant diver (including body, tank, and fins) completely removed from the image?
Is the entire bubble stream rising from the diver to the surface completely eliminated?
Is the edited area free of any residual specular highlights, sparkles, or silhouette traces of the removed objects?
Does the reconstructed water background exhibit a natural volumetric haze gradient and blue-green color shift consistent with underwater depth?
Are the suspended particles in the reconstructed area consistent in density, size, and randomness with the surrounding water?
Are distant coral or kelp silhouettes naturally reconstructed in the background where the diver was, avoiding empty or flat patches?
Is the local brightness in the edited area consistent with the surrounding water, showing no vertical streaks or unnatural shadows?
Are all foreground coral structures (including branching and brain corals) preserved exactly in their original positions and shapes?
Are the fine textures and colors of the foreground coral completely unaffected by the edit (no blurring or texture drift)?
Are all fish in the scene preserved in their exact original positions, orientations, and appearances?
Is the edited region free from visible seams, hard edges, or patch boundaries?
Is the reconstructed area free from repetitive cloning patterns or obvious 'stamp' artifacts?
Is the image free from AI hallucinations or unexpected new objects appearing in the edited region?
Does the edited area maintain the same noise, grain, and sharpness characteristics as the rest of the image?
